mechanism_narrative: A falling or very low FEDFUNDS trend raises S&P 500 forward returns by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite; the signal predicts positive 20-day returns when the policy-rate level is near the lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if lower or declining FEDFUNDS does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than higher or rising FEDFUNDS states in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high UNRATE level signals weak household income, default risk, and pressure on earnings expectations; when unemployment crosses into recessionary territory, the mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 8.0% have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or do not underperform non-triggered observations by at least 1 percentage point on average.

mechanism_narrative: Joint improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals a broad demand recovery in consumption and housing, raising expected revenues and cyclicals; simultaneous 3-month increases predict positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change crosses above 0 and HOUST 3-month change crosses above 0
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive 3-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods when only one series improves, or if interaction coefficient is not positive at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A deterioration in INDPRO signals weaker factory output, lower operating leverage, and falling earnings expectations for cyclical firms; a downward break in industrial production predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: Low CPIAUCSL inflation combined with FEDFUNDS near zero signals room for prolonged monetary accommodation without immediate inflation pressure, supporting valuation multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation is below 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-FEDFUNDS and low-inflation state does not outperform other monetary-inflation states over 60 trading days, or if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term is not positive in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: An improving PAYEMS trend signals that firms are expanding labor demand, which raises aggregate income and confirms earnings recovery; stronger payroll momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum fails to produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative PAYEMS momentum in walk-forward validation, or if the estimated slope is non-positive in more than half of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A negative GDPC1 growth break signals economy-wide demand contraction and weaker aggregate profits; when real GDP growth crosses below zero, the mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered negative GDPC1 growth observations do not underperform positive-growth observations over 60 trading days, or if the negative-growth hit rate for negative S&P 500 returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Very low HOUST combined with high UNRATE identifies a housing-led balance-sheet stress regime in which weak construction and labor income reinforce each other, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 700 and UNRATE crosses above 9.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-below-700 and UNRATE-above-9.0% trigger is not followed by lower 5-day S&P 500 returns than either condition alone, or if the joint-trigger mean return is non-negative.
